claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 21 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me ask you some questions to assess your eligibility.

**Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me how many children and their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit. I have three children - Alex who is 5, Blake who is 21, and Charlie who is 13.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! I can see you have three children: Alex (5), Blake (21), and Charlie (13).

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of them, I need to ask a few questions.

Let me start with **Blake, who is 21 years old**. Child Benefit normally stops when a child turns 16, unless they continue in approved education or training until they're under 20. 

**Is Blake currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Based on the rules, Blake at 21 years old and not in approved education or training would not qualify for Child Benefit, as eligibility typically ends at age 16 (or up to age 20 if in approved education or training).

Now let me ask about your other children. **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your living arrangements with your children.

**Do Alex (5) and Charlie (13) live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, both Alex and Charlie live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

**Are either Alex or Charlie currently in hospital, in care, or in any other living arrangement for an extended period (such as staying with someone else)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Charlie was in care for a little while recently, but it was only for about a month.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since Charlie was in care for about a month (which is less than 8 weeks), this shouldn't affect your eligibility for Child Benefit for Charlie.

Let me confirm I have all the information I need:
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex (5) and Charlie (13) both live with you
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them
- Charlie was in care for about a month (under 8 weeks)
- Blake (21) is not in education or training

**One last question: Has Charlie now returned home from care and is living with you again?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Charlie is back home with me now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on my assessment of your circumstances against the UK Child Benefit eligibility rules, here's my decision:

## Eligibility Summary:

**✓ Alex (5 years old) - ELIGIBLE**
You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for them, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

**✗ Blake (21 years old) - NOT ELIGIBLE**
You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake. Child Benefit normally stops when a child turns 16, unless they stay in approved education or training, in which case it can continue until they're under 20. Since Blake is 21 and not in education or training, they do not meet the eligibility criteria.

**✓ Charlie (13 years old) - ELIGIBLE**
You are eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie. Charlie is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for them, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. Although Charlie was in care for about a month, this is less than the 8-week threshold, and since Charlie is now back home with you, this does not affect your eligibility.

## Next Steps:
You can claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie. You would receive Child Benefit for two children.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for this child. Alex meets the age requirement (under 16) and all other eligibility criteria. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit eligibility normally ends when a child turns 16, unless they continue in approved education or training, in which case it can continue until they are under 20. Since Blake is 21 and not in education or training, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 13 years old and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Charlie, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for this child. Although Charlie was in care for about a month, this is less than the 8-week threshold after which Child Benefit would normally cease. Charlie has now returned home and lives with the claimant. Charlie meets the age requirement (under 16) and all other eligibility criteria. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with three children. Alex (5) and Charlie (13) both meet the eligibility criteria as they are under 16, live with the claimant, and nobody else is claiming for them. Charlie's brief period in care (about 1 month) is under the 8-week threshold and does not affect eligibility now that Charlie is back home. Blake (21) does not qualify as he is over the age limit (16, or under 20 if in approved education/training) and is not currently in education or training. The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, but not for Blake.

Blake (21) does not qualify as he is over the age limit (16, or under 20 if in approved education/training) and is not currently in education or training. The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, but not for Blake.
